claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Risk register identifies 11 risks; RISK-01 (medium/high, modular architecture + fallback modes from M6), RISK-04 (medium/high, ethics applications M1 + data abstraction layer), RISK-09 (low/high, deputy PI + consortium commitment clauses), RISK-10 (medium/high, shared API contracts M1–M3 + 6-monthly integration checkpoints)
Three governance bodies: General Assembly (all 8 partners, biannual), Project Management Committee (all 8 partners, quarterly), Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, minimum biannual)

Ethics self-assessment triggers Category 8 (AI, WP2/WP4/WP5/WP6), Category 4 (health data, WP5), Category 2 (clinical expert, WP5 T5-03); ATU holds Ethics Compliance Coordinator role; NIHS holds Healthcare Ethics Oversight role
8-partner consortium spanning 8 countries (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES), combining higher education institutions, research centres, and private organisations
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — partners[].country (ATU=DE, CERIA=FR, BIIS=NL, ISIA=IT, NIHS=SE, ELI=CH, BAL=FI, FIIT=ES); partners[].organisation_type (ATU/BIIS/ISIA/NIHS=HES; CERIA/FIIT=REC; ELI/BAL=PRC)

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Each partner holds exactly one WP lead role matched to their primary competence
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — partner_role_matrix[partner_id=ATU].wps_as_lead=[WP1,WP2] (contradicts claim; ATU holds two WP lead roles)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The claim states each partner holds exactly one WP lead role. wp_structure.json partner_role_matrix shows ATU.wps_as_lead = ['WP1','WP2'] — ATU holds two WP lead roles (WP1 and WP2). The referenced source directly contradicts the claim. All other partners hold one WP lead role. The claim requires correction: ATU holds two WP lead roles.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The consortium covers healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and logistics — described as 'the Apply AI sectors identified in SR-07'
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json — SR-07.description: 'The three specifically identified sectors are: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving' (logistics not listed as an SR-07 Apply AI sector; claim misattributes logistics to SR-07)
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: scope_requirements.json SR-07 identifies 'healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving' as the three specifically identified Apply AI sectors. Logistics is not an SR-07 Apply AI sector. WP7 is explicitly framed in wp_structure.json as a cross-sector transfer demonstrator 'beyond primary call sectors', not a primary call sector. The section's claim that logistics is one of 'the Apply AI sectors identified in SR-07' is contradicted by the Tier 2B source. The pre-existing CS-11 claim status ('confirmed') in the section's validation_status is incorrect — the Tier 2B source does not confirm logistics as an SR-07 Apply AI sector.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Budget gate passed; validated budget covers all 9 WPs and all 8 partners with no blocking inconsistencies
